model_narrative: We design a set of dice with integer face values so that they beat each other in a cycle, where each die tends to beat the next die in the cyclic order. For each die we decide the numeric value placed on every one of its faces, drawing those values from a shared pool of candidate numbers, and we decide which faces of a die are counted as beating which faces of the next die. We also track the common number of face-versus-face wins a die achieves against the next die in the cycle. The objective is to maximize that number of wins.

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the faces of a single die, listed in a fixed positional order from the first face to the last; this ordering is used to talk about adjacent faces

sets.name: d
sets.doc: the dice being designed, arranged in a fixed cyclic order; the die that follows the last die in this order wraps back around to the first die, which encodes the cyclic beating relationship the design aims for

params.name: wn
params.doc: the minimum number of wins targeted in the design; a scalar reference value of 19

params.name: big
params.doc: a single large constant used to switch off a face-comparison inequality when the corresponding comparison is not asserted; its magnitude equals the number of dice multiplied by the number of faces

params.name: fnum
params.doc: the pool of candidate numeric values, one slot per die-and-face position, that the design draws from when it assigns an actual numeric value to each die face; in the full data these range from 1 up to the number of dice times the number of faces

vars.name: wnx
vars.doc: the common number of face-versus-face wins each die scores against the next die in the cycle

vars.name: fval
vars.doc: the integer value placed on each face of each die, bounded between 1 and the number of dice times the number of faces; the first face of the first die is fixed to the value 1 to break symmetry

vars.name: comp
vars.doc: a face-comparison indicator that equals 1 when a given face of a die is asserted to beat a given face of the next die in the cycle, and 0 otherwise; the first face index refers to the current die and the second to the next die

vars.name: fmap
vars.doc: an assignment indicator that equals 1 when a particular die-face position draws its number from a particular slot of the value pool; the first die-face pair is the position being filled and the second die-face pair is the pool slot supplying the value

constraints.name: eq1
constraints.intent: For each die, the number of face-versus-face wins it is credited with against the next die in the cycle, counted across all pairs of one of its faces against one of the next die's faces, must equal the common win count we are tracking. In other words every die ends up with the same number of winning face matchups against its successor.

constraints.name:
d)
def
constraints.intent: Whenever a face of a die is asserted to beat a face of the next die in the cycle, the value on that die's face must actually exceed the value on the next die's face by at least one. This is enforced for every die together with every pairing of one of its faces against one of the next die's faces, but only when that particular face matchup is asserted as a win; when the matchup is not asserted the requirement is switched off by a large allowance so it places no real restriction.

constraints.name:
f)
def
constraints.intent: Within each die the face values must strictly increase from one face to the next in the fixed face order, so that going from any face to the one immediately after it the value goes up by at least one. This applies to every adjacent pair of faces on every die, which also guarantees the faces of a die all carry different values. The very first face has no face before it, so the requirement starts from the second face onward.

constraints.name: eq4
constraints.intent: The value placed on each die face must equal the number drawn from whichever single pool slot is selected to supply it. For every die-and-face position, summing each candidate pool value weighted by whether that pool slot is the one chosen for this position recovers the value assigned to that face.

constraints.name: eq5
constraints.intent: Every slot of the candidate value pool must be used exactly once across the whole design. For each pool slot, exactly one die-face position draws its value from that slot, so the assignment of pool values to face positions is a one-to-one matching.

query: We're looking at easing off the perfectly balanced set to see what it costs. There's a proposal on the table to put die 1 on a tighter win allowance against the die it faces in the cycle — its faces combined should land no more than a budget of the squared face-count minus three times the face-count of head-to-head wins. With that allowance in place, what common per-die win count does the optimizer return?
expected_pyomo: model.win_budget = Constraint(expr=sum(model.comp['dice1', f, fp] for f in model.f for fp in model.f) <= len(model.f) * len(model.f) - 3 * len(model.f))

query: Engineering is pitching a rule that keeps die 1 from feasting on the weak end of the die it faces: across all of die 1's faces, the total number of winning matchups landing on the next die's two lowest faces should be held to two at most. Plug that in and tell me the common per-die win count it works out to.
expected_pyomo: model.low_col_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.comp['dice1', f, fp] for f in model.f for fp in ['face1', 'face2']) <= 2)

query: There's a fairness idea we'd like to evaluate: no single face on any die should be allowed to sweep every face of the die it faces — each face has to drop at least one of those head-to-head matchups. Apply that across every die and face and walk me through the common win count it lands on.

query: We're considering a layout where die 2 is built to read as the stronger sibling of die 1 face for face — whatever value die 1 carries in a given face position, die 2 should carry at least as much in that same position, all the way up the ranking. What does the optimizer return for the common per-die win count under that pairing?

query: Every die in the plan runs up almost a clean sweep against the next die's lowest face, which feels like the easy wins are being double-counted. Shouldn't each die have to leave at least two of those matchups against the next die's weakest face on the table? Pin every die down to that and tell me what's keeping the model from making that move.

query: It bugs me that die 1 still scores off die 2's very weakest face — that's the matchup die 2 should never lose. Couldn't we just shut die 1 out entirely on die 2's lowest face, so none of die 1's faces beat it? What's the model seeing that makes it cling to those wins?
expected_pyomo: model.no_beat_low = Constraint(expr=sum(model.comp['dice1', f, 'face1'] for f in model.f) <= 0)

query: Die 2 looks like it's piling up cheap wins on the low end of die 3, the die it faces in the cycle. Wouldn't it be cleaner to hold die 2 to no more than two winning matchups across die 3's two lowest faces combined? Walk me through what's pulling the design the other way.
expected_pyomo: model.d2_low_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.comp['dice2', f, fp] for f in model.f for fp in ['face1', 'face2']) <= 2)

model_narrative: We design a set of dice with integer face values so that they beat each other in a cycle, where each die tends to beat the next die in the cyclic order. For each die we decide the numeric value placed on every one of its faces, drawing those values from a shared pool of candidate numbers, and we decide which faces of a die are counted as beating which faces of the next die. We also track the common number of face-versus-face wins a die achieves against the next die in the cycle. The objective is to maximize that number of wins.

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the faces of a single die, listed in a fixed positional order from the first face to the last; this ordering is used to talk about adjacent faces

sets.name: d
sets.doc: the dice being designed, arranged in a fixed cyclic order; the die that follows the last die in this order wraps back around to the first die, which encodes the cyclic beating relationship the design aims for

params.name: wn
params.doc: the minimum number of wins targeted in the design; a scalar reference value of 19

params.name: big
params.doc: a single large constant used to switch off a face-comparison inequality when the corresponding comparison is not asserted; its magnitude equals the number of dice multiplied by the number of faces

params.name: fnum
params.doc: the pool of candidate numeric values, one slot per die-and-face position, that the design draws from when it assigns an actual numeric value to each die face; in the full data these range from 1 up to the number of dice times the number of faces

vars.name: wnx
vars.doc: the common number of face-versus-face wins each die scores against the next die in the cycle

vars.name: fval
vars.doc: the integer value placed on each face of each die, bounded between 1 and the number of dice times the number of faces; the first face of the first die is fixed to the value 1 to break symmetry

vars.name: comp
vars.doc: a face-comparison indicator that equals 1 when a given face of a die is asserted to beat a given face of the next die in the cycle, and 0 otherwise; the first face index refers to the current die and the second to the next die

vars.name: fmap
vars.doc: an assignment indicator that equals 1 when a particular die-face position draws its number from a particular slot of the value pool; the first die-face pair is the position being filled and the second die-face pair is the pool slot supplying the value

constraints.name: eq1
constraints.intent: For each die, the number of face-versus-face wins it is credited with against the next die in the cycle, counted across all pairs of one of its faces against one of the next die's faces, must equal the common win count we are tracking. In other words every die ends up with the same number of winning face matchups against its successor.

constraints.name:
d)
def
constraints.intent: Whenever a face of a die is asserted to beat a face of the next die in the cycle, the value on that die's face must actually exceed the value on the next die's face by at least one. This is enforced for every die together with every pairing of one of its faces against one of the next die's faces, but only when that particular face matchup is asserted as a win; when the matchup is not asserted the requirement is switched off by a large allowance so it places no real restriction.

constraints.name:
f)
def
constraints.intent: Within each die the face values must strictly increase from one face to the next in the fixed face order, so that going from any face to the one immediately after it the value goes up by at least one. This applies to every adjacent pair of faces on every die, which also guarantees the faces of a die all carry different values. The very first face has no face before it, so the requirement starts from the second face onward.

constraints.name: eq4
constraints.intent: The value placed on each die face must equal the number drawn from whichever single pool slot is selected to supply it. For every die-and-face position, summing each candidate pool value weighted by whether that pool slot is the one chosen for this position recovers the value assigned to that face.

constraints.name: eq5
constraints.intent: Every slot of the candidate value pool must be used exactly once across the whole design. For each pool slot, exactly one die-face position draws its value from that slot, so the assignment of pool values to face positions is a one-to-one matching.

query: Two things look off at once: die 1 reads as strong as die 2 face for face, and a handful of faces are sweeping every matchup against the die they face. Couldn't we just impose both at once — make die 2 carry at least as much value as die 1 in every face position, and at the same time stop any face from beating all of the next die's faces? Pin both rules on together and tell me what's keeping the model from settling there on its own.
